events.title: Cyndi Lauper turns 73
events.blurb: American singer-songwriter and actress Cyndi Lauper, known for her distinctive image and powerful four-octave vocal range, celebrates her 73rd birthday.

angles.name: Girls Just Wanna Have Fun
angles.copy: It's Cyndi Lauper's 73rd birthday! Still as vibrant and unique as ever, what's your favourite hit from the 'Queen of Quirky Pop'?

events.title: Remembering George Michael, who would have been 63
events.blurb: English singer-songwriter and record producer George Michael, who rose to fame with Wham! and became a global solo superstar, would have celebrated his 63rd birthday.

angles.name: Faithful Memories
angles.copy: Thinking of the incredible George Michael today, who would have been 63. His music continues to inspire and move generations. What's your go-to George track?

events.title: 17 years since we lost Michael Jackson
events.blurb: June 25th marks 17 years since the passing of Michael Jackson, the 'King of Pop', who would have been 68 this year.

angles.name: King of Pop's Legacy
angles.copy: Seventeen years ago today, the world lost the incomparable Michael Jackson. His music, dance, and artistry remain unmatched. What's your most cherished MJ memory or song?

events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Richard Starkey, MBE, known professionally as Ringo Starr, the iconic drummer for The Beatles, celebrates his 86th birthday.

angles.name: Peace and Love Drummer
angles.copy: Happy 86th birthday to the legendary Ringo Starr! From the Fab Four to his solo career, his rhythm has defined generations. Send him some peace and love!

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Sir Brian Harold May, CBE, the legendary lead guitarist and songwriter of Queen, and accomplished astrophysicist, celebrates his 79th birthday.

angles.name: Guitar God's Birthday
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 79th birthday to the one and only Brian May! What's your favourite Queen guitar solo or track penned by the legend himself?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, the iconic lead singer and co-founder of The Rolling Stones, celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Still Got The Moves
angles.copy: Can you believe Mick Jagger is 83 today? The Rolling Stones frontman is still rocking stages worldwide. What's your favourite Jagger moment?

events.title: 45 years since MTV launched
events.blurb: On August 1, 1981, MTV launched with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its very first music video, changing the landscape of music and television forever.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago, MTV burst onto screens, forever changing how we consume music. Remember the first video they played? It was The Buggles! What's your favourite early MTV memory?

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' turns 32
events.blurb: Oasis released their groundbreaking debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which quickly became a Britpop defining record.

angles.name: Definitely a Classic
angles.copy: Thirty-two years ago today, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed British music forever. What's your favourite track from this iconic debut album?

events.title: 57 years since Woodstock Festival
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal event in music history and 1960s counterculture, took place from August 15-18, 1969.

angles.name: Peace, Love & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for '3 Days of Peace & Music' at Woodstock. What's your favourite performance from the legendary festival?

events.title: 49 years since the passing of Elvis Presley
events.blurb: August 16th marks 49 years since the death of the 'King of Rock and Roll', Elvis Presley, who would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: Long Live The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, 49 years after his passing. His voice and charisma remain timeless. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: 30 years since the death of Tupac Shakur
events.blurb: September 13th marks three decades since the tragic death of influential rapper Tupac Shakur, who would have been 55 this year.

angles.name: Tupac's Enduring Voice
angles.copy: Thirty years ago today, we lost a hip-hop icon, Tupac Shakur. His powerful lyrics and impact on music are still felt today. What's your favourite Tupac track?

events.title: 56 years since the passing of Jimi Hendrix
events.blurb: September 18th marks 56 years since the death of Jimi Hendrix, widely regarded as one of the greatest and most influential guitarists in history, who would have been 84.

angles.name: Guitar Legend Remembered
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled Jimi Hendrix, who passed away 56 years ago today. His innovative guitar work changed music forever. What's your favourite Hendrix riff?

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' turns 57
events.blurb: The Beatles released their eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', on September 26, 1969, featuring iconic tracks and its famous zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Iconic Album Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover is still one of the most famous in history. What's your favourite track from it?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: American-British singer, songwriter, and musician Chrissie Hynde, best known as the frontwoman of The Pretenders, celebrates her 75th birthday.

angles.name: Pretenders' Powerhouse
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the iconic Chrissie Hynde! Her distinctive voice and fierce stage presence have made The Pretenders rock legends. What's your favourite song?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: English singer and songwriter Liam Gallagher, known as the lead singer of Oasis and later Beady Eye and his solo career, celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star's Birthday
angles.copy: Our Kid is 54 today! Happy birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher. What's your favourite track featuring that unmistakable voice?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' album turns 55
events.blurb: John Lennon's critically acclaimed second solo album, 'Imagine', was released on October 8, 1971, featuring his signature peace anthem.

angles.name: Imagine The Impact
angles.copy: Fifty-five years ago, John Lennon released the 'Imagine' album, giving the world a timeless message of peace. What does this album mean to you?

events.title: 25 years since the first iPod was unveiled
events.blurb: Apple introduced the original iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music and promising '1,000 songs in your pocket'.

angles.name: Pocket Full of Music
angles.copy: Remember when you could fit 1,000 songs in your pocket? Twenty-five years ago, Apple unveiled the first iPod, changing how we listen to music forever.

events.title: Happy James Bond Day!
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Raise a martini (shaken, not stirred) to 007 and his incredible legacy. What's your favourite Bond film or theme tune?

events.title: Remembering John Lennon, who would have been 86
events.blurb: Legendary singer-songwriter and co-founder of The Beatles, John Lennon, would have celebrated his 86th birthday today.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the visionary John Lennon. His music and message of peace continue to resonate globally.
